query: I’m not sure of the reservation ID off the top of my head, but it was the business flight I had booked earlier this month. The cancellation was definitely by the airline—I got a notification just a few hours before departure. I had to scramble to make other arrangements, and I ended up missing my meeting entirely. I really think I deserve compensation for all the inconvenience and the missed opportunity. Isn’t there something you can do for me?

state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: The user says an earlier business flight was canceled by the airline, caused them to miss an important meeting, and asks for compensation.
assistant: The assistant asks for the user ID and reservation ID.
user: The user provides user ID sophia_silva_7557, says the reservation ID is unavailable, and asks the assistant to check the account.
assistant: The assistant retrieves the account and reviews the reservations visible under sophia_silva_7557.
assistant: After listing the visible bookings, the assistant asks the user to identify which reservation matches the canceled business flight and to confirm the airline canceled it.
user: The user says they do not know the reservation ID, reiterates that the airline canceled the trip shortly before departure, explains the missed meeting, and asks whether anything can be done.

candidate_answers.text: I understand why you’re upset. Since I still can’t identify which reservation this refers to from the visible bookings, the next grounded step is to clarify the reservation details rather than promise compensation.

The user is restating the cancellation impact but still has not supplied a reservation ID, so retrieving more reservation-specific detail is the best supported move before deciding on compensation or escalation.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Use the later user turn that follows the account lookup but still occurs before the explicit escalation request, when the user has confirmed an airline-caused cancellation yet still cannot identify the reservation.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from the decision-point state. Scores reflect which action most directly advances the user's request using only visible conversation evidence.
notes: query exactly matches the selected user message at index 13
history_summary stops at the decision point and excludes the assistant response that questions the account data and all later transfer outputs
candidate_answers are manual non-leaky drafts grounded only in visible context
selected_message_index is 13 because this turn is a real user message after account lookup but before explicit escalation, and its provenance tuple is unused elsewhere
special case: the visible reservations do not yet identify the claimed canceled business-class trip, so reservation-specific retrieval is more grounded than granting compensation immediately
